evidence: The amber query vector reorients across frames while the periwinkle keys hold still, and the tallest lilac bar (leading key) tracks the query, giving a single covariation focus.
The frame shows one query, four keys, and their four paired score bars — a single coherent system, not five independent novel ideas.
Labels q and k1..k4 sit directly on their arrow tips, and each numeric readout sits under its own bar; no legend or swatch key appears.
On-screen text is limited to object labels and numeric score readouts beside their bars; no prose block sits alone on the background.
The q/k labels and the +0.98/+1.00 style readouts are rendered at a large, easily readable size relative to frame height.
All arrows, labels, bars, and readouts sit fully inside the frame with comfortable margins, including the rightmost k4 group.
Each label, arrow, bar, and readout occupies its own column with no overlap or stacking across the frames.
The query is amber (attention/heat), the keys are periwinkle (observed), and the score bars are lilac, matching Night School token semantics.
Every element — query, keys, bars, readouts — carries propositional content; no mascots or decorative filler are present.

The amber query sweeps direction across frames while the four periwinkle keys stay fixed and the lilac score bars rise/fall with alignment (e.g. k1 leads when q points left, k3/k4 lead when q rotates right), delivering the promised covariation.
Each key's arrow, its bar, and its numeric readout are stacked in a single vertical column, keeping related items together.
Across frames the query rotates continuously and bar heights morph smoothly with the score values, reading as motion rather than cuts.
The composition has generous negative space with evenly spaced columns and a clear left-to-right reading order.
